content.problemStatement:
There are 222 trains AAA and BBB and NNN stations in a line from 111 to NNN in order. There is also an array PPP of length N−1N-1N−1 such that PiP_iPi​ (1≤i<N)(1\le i \lt N)(1≤i<N) denotes the amount of time any train takes to go from the iii-th station to the (i+1)(i+1)(i+1)-th station.

Initially, both trains are at station 111. Train AAA departs from station 111 and stops directly at station NNN. For safety purposes, it is maintained that train BBB cannot depart from station iii unless train AAA has already reached station (i+1)(i+1)(i+1) (1≤i<N)(1 \le i \lt N)(1≤i<N).

Find the **minimum** time after which train BBB reaches station NNN, once train AAA departs from station 111.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN, denoting number of stations.
The next line contains N−1N-1N−1 space-separated integers, P1,P2,…,PN−1P_1,P_2,\ldots ,P_{N-1}P1​,P2​,…,PN−1​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum time after which train BBB reaches station NNN.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10510^5105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: AAA reaches station 222 at t=4t=4t=4 and at this time BBB departs from station 111 and reaches station 222 at t=4+4=8t=4+4=8t=4+4=8.
Test case 222: Following is the timeline of two trains-

At t=3t=3t=3, AAA reaches station 222 and BBB departs from station 111.
At t=6t=6t=6, BBB reaches station 222 but AAA has not yet reached station 333, so train BBB will wait at station 222.
At t=8t=8t=8, AAA reaches station 333 and BBB departs from station 222.
At t=8+5=13t=8+5=13t=8+5=13, train BBB reaches station 333.
